summary.intent: Implement a filter in linked-doc resolution that excludes skill sub-files, keeping only SKILL.md at the root of each skill folder and discarding progressive-disclosure reference files nested within skill directories.
summary.outcome: Successfully added isSkillsSubFile() helper and two continue guards in docs-finder.ts, plus 5 new test cases in docs-finder.test.ts. All 28 tests pass, lint and typecheck clean. Changes committed.

repo: Skills follow the structure <root>/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md; sub-files are progressive-disclosure refs not meant for agent context
Linked-doc filtering is the correct stage for this logic, matching where isSelfReference() already operates for AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md self-links
There is a pre-existing failing test in evaluation routes unrelated to docs-finder
The project uses the `rtk` prefix wrapper for git commands but not for bun commands

code.finding: isSelfReference() is the existing precedent for inline filtering at linked-doc resolution stage; isSkillsSubFile() was added directly after it
absolutePath is resolved here in the inline link loop; filter guard must be inserted immediately after this line
absolutePath is resolved here in the reference-definition loop; identical filter guard needed in this second loop

workflow: Running a specific test file requires `bunx --bun bun test <file>` syntax, not `bun run test <file>`
JSDoc comments containing `*/` as a glob example (e.g. `*/skills/`) prematurely close the block comment and cause a syntax error; escape or reformat such patterns

friction: JSDoc comment for isSkillsSubFile() used `*/` as a glob pattern example which terminated the block comment early, requiring a follow-up fix
Initial test invocation with `rtk bun run test` failed; had to fall back to `bun run test` directly
Running tests scoped to a single file required discovering the `bunx --bun bun test` invocation pattern rather than the standard `bun run test` form
open_items: Pre-existing failing test in evaluation routes was noted but not investigated or fixed
